title: Tackling Homelessness in Los Angeles
subtitle: 为何洛杉矶街头住满了人

paragraphs.en: There are close to 58,000 homeless people in Los Angeles county. That's a shocking and tragic number for a region that's home to some of the richest people in the world. At last, the problem became so acute — and so visible — that Los Angeles took extraordinary action. The citizens of this county voted in November 2016 and again in March 2017 to raise their own taxes. The purpose of such action is to fund an enormous multibillion-dollar 10-year program of housing and social services for the homeless.
paragraphs.zh: 洛杉矶县有近五万八千名无家可归者。对于一个居住着全球部分顶级富豪的地区来说，这一数字令人震惊且充满悲剧色彩。问题终于变得如此严峻和显眼，以至于洛杉矶采取了非常行动。该县选民分别在2016年11月和2017年3月投票同意增加税收，目的是为一项耗资数十亿美元、为期十年的无家可归者住房与社会服务计划提供资金。

paragraphs.en: As a result, Los Angeles now has its best chance in decades to combat homelessness. This is an opportunity that surely all can agree must not be wasted. It is neither desirable nor morally acceptable nor practical for this county to tolerate the signs of deprivation more commonly associated with the slums of Rio de Janiero. How did we get here? For as long as there are homeless people, there is a tendency to blame the victims themselves for their condition — to see their failure to thrive as an issue of character, of moral weakness, of laziness.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，洛杉矶如今迎来了数十年来解决无家可归问题的最佳机遇。这一机遇绝不应被浪费，想必人人都会认同这一点。对洛杉矶县而言，容忍那些通常与里约热内卢贫民窟联系在一起的贫困迹象，既不可取，道德上也难容，实际操作中亦不可行。我们是如何走到这一步的？只要无家可归者依然存在，人们就倾向于将他们的处境归咎于受害者自身——将他们的困顿视作性格缺陷、道德软弱或懒惰所致。

paragraphs.en: But contrary to popular belief, the homeless in Los Angeles are not mostly mentally ill or drug addicted or frightening, although a sizable minority meet some of those descriptions. Today, a greater and greater proportion of people living on the streets are there because of bad luck or a series of mistakes, or because the economy forgot them — they lost a job or were evicted or fled an abusive marriage just as the housing market was growing increasingly unforgiving. The challenges are enormous, even if everyone is pulling in the same direction.
paragraphs.zh: 但与普遍看法相反，洛杉矶的无家可归者大多并非精神疾病患者、瘾君子或危险人物，尽管确有相当一部分人符合其中某些描述。如今，越来越多流落街头的人是因为运气不佳、一连串的失误，或是被经济所遗忘——他们失业了、被驱逐了，或是在房地产市场日益冷酷无情之际逃离了充满暴力的婚姻。即便所有人都同心协力，面临的挑战依然十分艰巨。

paragraphs.en: That reality was driven home this month by a new Los Angeles Homeless Services Authority report showing that the county's homeless population is growing faster than the supply of new housing. In the world's richest nation, homelessness on this scale should be shameful and shocking. But most Los Angeles residents are no longer either shocked or shamed. Increasingly, we are uncomfortable, annoyed, disgusted, scared or unaware. Compassion is being replaced by resignation.
paragraphs.zh: 本月，洛杉矶无家可归者服务管理局发布的一份新报告让这一现实更加触目惊心：该县无家可归人口的增长速度已超过新增住房的供应速度。在世界上最富有的国家，如此规模的无家可归现象本该令人羞愧和震惊。然而，大多数洛杉矶居民已不再感到震惊或羞愧。取而代之的，是愈发普遍的不适、厌烦、反感、恐惧或漠然。同情心正逐渐被听天由命的无奈所取代。

words.meaning: phr. 使人充分理解；讲清楚
